clues.clue: 'Snap, Crackle, Pop' are the three elf mascots of this crispy rice cereal.
'Kid tested, mother approved' has long pitched this General Mills cereal.
'The Breakfast of Champions' is the boast of this athlete-endorsed wheat-flake cereal.
'Follow your nose' guides Toucan Sam to this Kellogg's fruity-ring cereal.
'They're magically delicious' describes the marshmallows in this Lucky charm cereal.
This New York baseball club, the 'Bronx Bombers,' has won 27 World Series.
This Green Bay NFL team takes its name from a meatpacking sponsor.
This Boston NBA team sports a leprechaun logo and 17 banners.
Fans of this 'Steel City' NFL team wave Terrible Towels.
This Pittsburgh NHL club, 'the Pens,' has won multiple Stanley Cups.
In this island game, players gather brick, wood, sheep, wheat and ore to build settlements.
In this cooperative game, players race to cure four diseases sweeping the globe.
In this game, players collect colored cards to claim cross-country rail routes.
In this tile-laying game, you place 'meeples' on a growing medieval French landscape.
In this hidden-role party game, villagers try to unmask the secret lycanthropes among them.
These ring-shaped oat pieces in a yellow box are pitched as heart-healthy.
These frosted, shredded-wheat pillows come in a 'bite size' version.
These sugar-dusted square pieces are hawked by mischievous cartoon bakers.
These pieces are shaped like the 26 characters of the alphabet.
These fruity pebble-shaped bits are tied to a Stone Age cartoon family.
This blazing star sits at the center of our solar system.
This is Earth's one and only natural satellite.
A solar one of these happens when our satellite passes directly in front of the Sun.
The Sun is composed mostly of this lightest chemical element.
The Moon's gravity is the main driver of these twice-daily ocean rises and falls.

final.clue: The 1939 classic 'The Wizard of Oz' ends with Dorothy clicking her heels and repeating 'there's no place like' this.
